title: Searsia dissecta
intro: Searsia dissecta, also known as the bitterbush or yellow-fruit bitterbush, is a small, deciduous shrub native to the southern parts of Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Searsia dissecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Searsia dissecta is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.

It is a member of the Anacardiaceae family, which is a group of plants known for their fruits and seeds that contain a caustic resin.
